senses.definition: a toy for children that usually looks like a small baby

examples.x: My baby hugged his soft, plush doll tightly while sleeping.
My friend had a doll with a voice recorder that could say different phrases.
Sarah enjoyed brushing her doll's hair and styling it.
She dressed up her doll in a beautiful pink dress.
We organized a tea party for our dolls with tiny cups and saucers.

senses.definition: a doll with a hollow head and a cloth body, designed to be worn over the hand and controlled with fingers

examples.x: The child played with a colorful puppet during storytime.
The teacher used a puppet to make the lesson more engaging.
She used a puppet to entertain the kids at the party.
He bought a handmade puppet from the craft fair.
The puppet’s mouth moved as she spoke through it.

senses.definition: a small three-dimensional representation of a character, animal, or object, often made of plastic, metal, or other materials, used for play or display as part of a collection or toy set

examples.x: She proudly displayed her collection of toy figurines on the shelf.
I found an old toy figurine from my childhood while cleaning the attic.
He loves using his toy figurines to act out stories and adventures.
I bought a toy figurine of my favorite superhero to add to my collection.
They decided to buy a toy figurine as a gift for their niece's birthday.

senses.definition: a toy that usually looks like an animal and is covered and filled with soft materials

examples.x: She carries her favorite stuffed toy everywhere she goes.
The store had a huge selection of stuffed toys for all ages.
My little brother always sleeps with his stuffed toy at night.
The children lined up to pick their favorite stuffed toy at the fair.
She hugged her stuffed toy tightly as she watched the movie.

senses.definition: a set of wooden dolls of decreasing sizes placed one inside the other, with the smallest doll containing all the others, representing a traditional and iconic Russian toy

examples.x: She carefully stacked the matryoshka dolls, one inside the other.
I bought a beautiful matryoshka doll as a souvenir from my trip to Russia.
My grandmother has a collection of matryoshka dolls from different countries.
He gave her a matryoshka doll set for her birthday, which she loved displaying on her shelf.

senses.definition: a small toy house often containing tiny furniture and small dolls as well
a fashion doll characterized by her oversized head, large eyes that change colors with a pull-string mechanism, and her ability to change facial expressions, making her a unique and collectible doll

examples.x: She spent hours customizing her Blythe doll, changing its clothes and styling its hair.
She found a rare Blythe doll at the flea market and couldn’t wait to add it to her collection.
The Blythe doll collection on the shelf caught everyone's attention with its large, colorful eyes.

senses.definition: a type of doll, often made of plastic, with an emphasis on style and clothing, designed for imaginative play and typically representing teenage or adult female characters

examples.x: She spent the afternoon dressing her fashion dolls in different outfits.
He bought his sister a new fashion doll for her birthday.
The fashion doll’s hair was styled in a neat ponytail.
My daughter loves to play with her fashion dolls and create new outfits for them.

senses.definition: a type of doll, usually made of plastic or cloth, designed to resemble an infant or young child and intended for nurturing and role-playing activities
a type of doll that, based on popular belief, represents an attractive young woman

examples.x: She dressed her Barbie doll in a sparkly evening gown for the party.
He surprised her with a vintage Barbie doll from the 1980s.
The Barbie doll came with a set of mini accessories for her dream house.
The new Barbie doll set included a car and a pet dog.

senses.definition: a fashion doll character manufactured by Mattel and designed to be the male counterpart to Barbie, featuring a diverse range of professions and styles

examples.x: My little sister has a collection of Barbie dolls, and she just added a Ken doll to her set.
The Ken doll comes with various outfits, like a suit or casual wear.
The new Ken doll looks different with his updated hairstyle and facial features.
I found an old Ken doll at a garage sale and decided to add it to my collection.

senses.definition: a posable toy figurine, often made of plastic, representing characters from movies, TV shows, comics, or other media, designed for imaginative play and collectability

examples.x: The new action figure set comes with a variety of weapons and gear.
My brother and I spent the afternoon creating epic battles with our action figures.
He arranged his action figures neatly on a shelf, showcasing them like a mini museum.
Sarah arranged her action figures on the shelf, placing each one in a cool pose.
Tim loves collecting action figures from his favorite superhero movies.

senses.definition: a human-like soft doll made from cloth pieces

examples.x: The little girl hugged her favorite rag doll tightly at bedtime.
They made a rag doll together during the craft class.
Her grandmother gifted her a handmade rag doll for her birthday.
The rag doll had colorful patches stitched all over it.
The rag doll was so worn out that its stitching began to unravel.

senses.definition: a small three-dimensional representation of an animal, often made of plastic, ceramic, or other materials, used for decorative display, collecting, or imaginative play

examples.x: The child carefully arranged the animal figurines on the shelf to create a pretend zoo.
The teacher used animal figurines during the lesson to teach the students about wildlife.
She played with her animal figurines, pretending they were having a meeting in the forest.
The animal figurines were scattered across the floor after an afternoon of imaginative play.

senses.definition: a small figurine or miniature replica of a military soldier, typically made of plastic, metal, or other materials, used for playing war games, collecting, or as decorative items

examples.x: The child carefully lined up his toy soldiers on the table to prepare for an imaginary battle.
A collection of antique toy soldiers was displayed on the shelf in the study.
He used toy soldiers to recreate a historical battle he had read about in school.
After playing all afternoon, he put the toy soldiers back into their storage box.
The toy soldiers came with tiny cannons and flags to make the scenes more realistic.

senses.definition: a Muslim fashion doll created as an alternative to Barbie, designed with Islamic values and modesty in mind

examples.x: My younger sister loves playing with her Fulla doll, especially when she dresses her up in different outfits.
I found a Fulla doll with a beautiful pink abaya for my niece's birthday.
My friend gave me a Fulla doll as a gift, and it was so nice to see a doll that represents my culture.

senses.definition: a type of doll that represents characters, people, or cultural elements from various African regions or communities, reflecting the diversity and rich cultural heritage of the African continent
a type of doll with a flat, paddle-like body and a simple design, often made of wood or other materials, used in various cultures for ceremonial, spiritual, or play purposes

examples.x: The children played with their paddle dolls, creating stories about their little wooden characters.
She found an old paddle doll in the attic, reminding her of her childhood.
The paddle doll was simple, yet its charm made it a favorite in the toy collection.
At the craft fair, there were many handcrafted paddle dolls for sale, each with a unique design.
He used a paddle doll to show his younger sister how to make up stories.

senses.definition: a character or doll that originated in the late 19th century, featuring a black-skinned figure with exaggerated features and clothing, but it has been considered controversial and offensive due to its racially insensitive portrayal of black individuals

examples.x: Many collectors seek antique golliwog dolls, unaware of the offensive implications they carry in modern times.
The golliwog was often featured in books and illustrations, contributing to negative stereotypes of Black people.
The golliwog was once a staple in many households, but today it is regarded as a symbol of racism.

senses.definition: a handmade toy typically crafted by the Amish community, representing a simple, traditional style, and often reflecting the cultural values and heritage of the Amish people
a type of folk art doll made from dried apples that have been carved and shaped into a doll-like form, often dressed with fabric and accessories, and used as a decorative item or a craft project

examples.x: She made a charming apple doll by drying an apple and dressing it in fabric clothes.
The children loved playing with their apple dolls during the fall festival.
My grandmother showed me how to create an apple doll using simple materials.
The apple doll sat on the shelf, adding a rustic touch to the room's decor.
At the craft fair, they sold handmade apple dolls as unique gifts.

senses.definition: a small doll or figurine, usually made of cloth or other materials, used in various cultures and practices for ritual, magical, or healing purposes, and it can represent a person or deity

examples.x: She made a poppet out of fabric and yarn for her art project.
The old book mentioned that poppets were used in ancient rituals.
The witch kept a poppet on her shelf as part of her collection of mystical items.
In some cultures, poppets are believed to hold special powers or magic.

senses.definition: a traditional handcrafted doll made by the Inuit people of the Arctic region, representing cultural and spiritual significance, and often reflecting the clothing, lifestyle, and customs of the Inuit community

examples.x: The Inuit doll was beautifully crafted, with its fur-lined parka and tiny boots.
She collected a variety of dolls, including an authentic Inuit doll made from seal skin.
As a child, he loved playing with his Inuit doll, pretending to live in the frozen tundra.
The teacher used an Inuit doll to show the students how people in the Arctic adapted to cold climates.

senses.definition: a traditional hand-carved and painted doll made by the Hopi and other Pueblo Native American tribes, representing various spiritual and cultural figures, and used in religious ceremonies and as art objects

examples.x: I learned that kachina dolls are not just decorative, but have deep cultural meaning.
The artist spent hours carving and painting the kachina doll to make it as authentic as possible.
During the ceremony, the elders used the kachina dolls to teach the children about their traditions.
She carefully placed the kachina doll on the shelf as part of her collection.

senses.definition: a traditional handmade doll created by Native American and other indigenous cultures, made from dried corn husks or corn leaves and crafted into doll-like shapes

examples.x: My grandmother made a corn husk doll for me when I was a child.
We used corn husk dolls as decorations for our fall festival.
The children loved playing with their handmade corn husk dolls in the garden.
During the harvest season, many families create corn husk dolls as a way to honor the crops.
I found an old corn husk doll in the attic, reminding me of family traditions.

senses.definition: a simple and often homemade doll made from a wooden clothespin or peg, typically decorated with fabric, paint, and other materials, and used for play or as a craft project

examples.x: She played with her peg doll, imagining it as a character in a grand adventure.
I remember making peg dolls for my school project last year.
The children loved setting up a little village with their peg dolls.
He painted the peg doll with bright colors and gave it a tiny fabric dress.
My grandmother gave me a set of peg dolls, and I still keep them on my shelf.

senses.definition: a traditional Japanese wooden doll with a cylindrical body and a simple hand-painted design, originating from specific regions in Japan and valued as both folk art and collectible items

examples.x: She collected kokeshi dolls from every region of Japan during her travels.
The kokeshi doll on the shelf has bright red flowers painted on its kimono.
My grandmother gave me a beautiful kokeshi doll as a gift when I visited Japan.
After visiting the museum, I bought a handmade kokeshi doll to remember the experience.

senses.definition: a traditional wooden doll from the southern region of India, typically made from teakwood and adorned with vibrant clothing, representing a significant cultural symbol and often given as a gift during weddings and festivals

a type of porcelain doll made with unglazed white bisque or parian ware, known for its smooth and marble-like appearance, and it was popular during the Victorian era for its elegant and realistic features

examples.x: The parian doll on display in the museum had exquisite details on its face and dress.
I found an old parian doll at a garage sale, and it was in surprisingly good condition.
My grandmother's collection includes a parian doll that was passed down from her mother.
The antique store had a beautiful parian doll, but it was too expensive for me to buy.

senses.definition: a type of doll made from unglazed, fine-quality porcelain or bisque ceramic, known for its delicate and lifelike appearance, often used as collectibles or playthings during the late 19th and early 20th centuries

examples.x: She has a collection of antique bisque dolls from the 1900s.
The bisque doll on the shelf has intricate details, especially around the eyes and mouth.
I found a beautiful bisque doll at the flea market yesterday, and it’s in great condition.
When I was younger, I loved playing with my bisque dolls, admiring their realistic faces.

senses.definition: a type of small, unglazed porcelain doll dating back to the mid-19th century, representing a young girl in a standing position, often used as party favors or toys in Victorian times and valued as collectible items today

examples.x: I found a Frozen Charlotte at the antique shop; it was so tiny and delicate.
She collects Frozen Charlotte dolls and has an impressive collection displayed in her living room.
As a child, she had a small Frozen Charlotte, which she kept on her shelf as a keepsake.
The museum had an exhibit on Victorian toys, and there was a display of Frozen Charlotte dolls.

senses.definition: a lifelike doll that is meticulously handcrafted to resemble a real human baby, often made with vinyl or silicone materials, and it is a popular collectible among doll enthusiasts and used for therapeutic purposes or artistic display

examples.x: She carefully placed her reborn baby doll in the crib to make it look like a real infant.
Some people find comfort in caring for a reborn baby doll as part of their therapy.
The reborn baby doll was painted with such fine detail that it looked like a real newborn.
My niece loves playing with her reborn baby doll, treating it like a real baby.

senses.definition: a collectible figurine with a spring-mounted head that bobbles or nods when the body is tapped or moved, often depicting famous personalities, characters, or athletes, and used for display or as novelty items

examples.x: I bought a bobblehead of my favorite football player to put on my desk.
The bobblehead on the dashboard of his car bounced as he drove over the bumps.
Every time I walk by the shelf, the bobblehead of the singer makes me smile.
The kids had fun watching the bobblehead’s head wobble as they shook it gently.

senses.definition: a type of doll with articulated joints held together by ball-and-socket connections, allowing for a wide range of posability and customization, and it is popular among collectors and hobbyists for its expressive and lifelike features

examples.x: She spent hours customizing her ball-jointed doll with a new outfit and wig.
The ball-jointed doll's intricate details made it a favorite among collectors.
He carefully posed his ball-jointed doll for the photo shoot.
The ball-jointed doll's flexible joints allow it to sit, stand, and move in many different positions.

senses.definition: a Japanese term for a paper doll or figurine used in Shinto rituals and ceremonies, representing a deity or spirit and serving as a spiritual medium or placeholder for the presence of the divine during religious practices

examples.x: The Shinto priest used a katashiro to cleanse the house of negative energy during the ceremony.
After the katashiro was passed over, it was burned to symbolize the removal of bad spirits.
We learned about the katashiro in our Japanese culture class and how it is used in purification rituals.
The katashiro served as a reminder of the importance of spiritual purity in Japanese customs.
At the festival, they handed out small katashiro for people to use in a traditional cleansing practice.

senses.definition: a doll that is believed through which one can control or harm someone else

examples.x: The practitioner used a voodoo doll to perform a healing ritual for a sick friend.
Legends tell of voodoo dolls being used to curse enemies or protect loved ones from harm.
The voodoo priestess created a personalized voodoo doll as part of a ritual to bring prosperity to a client's business.
The belief in voodoo dolls varies among practitioners, with some seeing them as potent tools for positive or negative magic.
In popular culture, voodoo dolls are often depicted as having mystical powers to manipulate people or events.

senses.definition: a toy doll characterized by its brightly colored hair, large eyes, and small, pudgy body, originally created in the 1960s and known for its popularity and nostalgic value, often collected or used for play

examples.x: I found an old troll doll in my attic while cleaning.
My sister used to play with her troll dolls for hours when we were kids.
She has a collection of troll dolls with all different hair colors.
Troll dolls were a popular toy back in the 90s and are still collected today.

senses.definition: a type of doll made from a mixture of materials, including glue, sawdust, and other substances, shaped and molded into doll forms, typically produced during the early to mid-20th century and cherished for their vintage charm and collectible value

examples.x: My grandmother's old composition doll is still in great condition.
Composition dolls were common before plastic dolls became popular.
I found a beautiful composition doll at the flea market yesterday.
The antique shop had several beautiful composition dolls on display.

senses.definition: a weighted or rounded-bottom toy with a weighted base, designed to wobble and return to an upright position after being pushed or knocked over, providing entertainment and amusement for children

examples.x: The baby giggled as the roly-poly toy rolled across the floor and wobbled back up.
The children took turns pushing the roly-poly toy to see how fast it would roll.
I bought a colorful roly-poly toy for my niece, and she loves watching it move.
My toddler was fascinated by the way the roly-poly toy always returned to its feet.
The roly-poly toy was one of the first toys that kept my baby entertained for hours.

senses.definition: a traditional articulated toy made from paper or wood, featuring a jointed design that allows the arms and legs to move when pulled by strings or levers, creating a jumping or dancing motion when manipulated
a simple and typically two-dimensional doll made from paper or cardboard, often printed with clothing and accessories, that can be cut out and dressed up for play or creative activities

examples.x: She spent hours playing with her paper dolls, changing their outfits every few minutes.
We made our own paper dolls in art class and colored them in with markers.
As a child, I loved dressing up my paper dolls in all kinds of fancy clothes.
The paper doll set came with a booklet showing different outfits to try.
I remember cutting out the paper dolls and carefully folding the little tabs to make them stand up.

senses.definition: a popular line of plush toy dolls in the 1980s, designed to look like simple characters with a face on a pillow-shaped body, often used as pillows or cuddly companions for children

examples.x: I remember carrying around my favorite Pillow People doll everywhere when I was little.

senses.definition: a toy that looks like a bear and is made of soft materials

examples.x: She received a soft teddy bear as a birthday gift, which quickly became her favorite cuddle companion.
The children brought their teddy bears to the picnic, turning it into a delightful teddy bear tea party.
He donated a large teddy bear to the local children's hospital to brighten the day of young patients.
The teddy bear was wearing a little blue shirt, making it even more adorable.
The store sells teddy bears in different colors.

senses.definition: a small toy character or creature, designed with a simple and often chubby appearance, often used for decorative or collectible purposes

examples.x: I found a cute Gonk at the thrift store, and it instantly brightened my day.
My grandmother gave me a Gonk as a gift, and it always makes me smile.
The Gonk's fuzzy body and oversized eyes make it look so silly.
The Gonk I bought yesterday has a colorful hat and makes a quirky addition to my collection.

senses.definition: a traditional handmade toy made from socks, typically featuring a red-heeled design, with button eyes, and a stitched-on mouth, known for its charming and nostalgic appearance

examples.x: My grandmother made me a sock monkey when I was little, and I still have it on my shelf.
We spent the afternoon making sock monkeys as a fun craft project.
The sock monkey's button eyes gave it such a cute, playful look.
I found an old sock monkey at a thrift store and couldn't resist buying it.

senses.definition: a type of puppet controlled by rods or strings attached to its body, used in puppetry performances and storytelling

examples.x: The puppeteer skillfully moved the rod puppet to make it appear as though it was dancing.
I watched a fun puppet show where the characters were all rod puppets, each with a unique set of movements.
The rod puppet’s head turned as the puppeteer gently pulled the rod, making it seem like the character was looking around.
In the theater production, the rod puppets interacted with the human actors, creating a magical experience.
He used a rod puppet to act out a funny skit during the school play.

senses.definition: a traditional Japanese puppet used in Bunraku theater, operated by three puppeteers to control its movements

examples.x: The craftsmanship of a bunraku puppet is amazing, with each one carefully made to show a wide range of emotions.
The bunraku puppets were dressed in beautiful, detailed costumes that made the performance even more impressive.
The puppeteer skillfully controlled the bunraku puppet, making its movements look smooth and lifelike.

senses.definition: a small puppet that fits onto a single finger, with the puppeteer's hand forming the character's body
a kind of puppet that fits over the hand and resembles a person or an animal
a puppet controlled by strings or wires attached to its limbs, manipulated by a puppeteer to perform intricate movements and actions

examples.x: The puppeteer skillfully controlled the marionette, making it dance across the stage.

At the theater, the audience was amazed by how lifelike the marionette seemed when it walked and waved.

senses.definition: a traditional folk toy or puppet with movable limbs, typically made of wood, used in dancing or rhythmic performances, where the puppeteer manipulates the strings or wires to create lively movements

examples.x: The children were delighted to watch the jig doll dance as they turned the handle.
At the antique shop, I found a beautiful wooden jig doll from the 1800s.
When I was younger, I would often play with my jig doll, making it dance to my favorite tunes.

senses.definition: a type of puppet or prop with a large head, often made of foam or fabric, mounted on a stick or handle, used in puppetry, theater, or entertainment performances for comedic or dramatic effect

examples.x: The jester entertained the crowd with his marotte, making it appear as if it were speaking and reacting to his antics.
In the old play, the actor used a marotte to mock the king, adding humor to the scene.
The marotte was a colorful and amusing accessory that added to the performer’s costume.
At the museum, there was a display of antique marottes, showcasing the craftsmanship of past puppet makers.

senses.definition: a small-scale version of a real object or item, often used for play, collection, or display purposes, and it is typically designed to replicate the details and features of the original on a smaller scale

examples.x: She collected miniature toys from all over the world and displayed them in a glass case.
The store had a collection of miniature toys that were perfect for collectors.
They used miniature toys to create a tiny world on the table for their storytelling game.
My grandfather used to have a shelf full of miniature toy trains from when he was a child.
